paper.exam: B.Tech./B.Tech. (Working Professional) IV Semester Examination, June 2025
paper.note:
i) Attempt any five questions.
ii) All questions carry equal marks.

parts.label: a
parts.text: Write a recursive algorithm for computing the nth Fibonacci number.

parts.label: b
parts.text: A machine needs a minimum of 200 sec to sort 1000 elements by Quick sort. What is the minimum time needed to sort 200 elements will be approximately?

parts.label: a
parts.text: Generate recurrence relation from the recursive binary search algorithm.

parts.label: b
parts.text: Consider the problem of finding the smallest and largest elements in an array of numbers using divide and Conquer method.

parts.label: a
parts.text: Find the time complexity of the recurrence relation T(n)=n+T(n/10)+T(7n/5).

parts.label: b
parts.text: Analyze the time complexity of Strassen's algorithm compared to conventional matrix multiplication.

parts.label: a
parts.text: What is Minimum Cost Spanning Tree? Explain Kruskal's Algorithm with the help of algorithm.

parts.label: b
parts.text: The recurrence T(n)=7T(n/2)+n² describe the running time of an algorithm A. A competing algorithm A has a running time of Γ(n)=aΓ(n/4)+n². What is the largest integer value for a A' is asymptotically faster than A?

parts.label: a
parts.text: Explain properties of Binomial Heap. Write an algorithm to perform uniting two Binomial Heaps. And also, to find Minimum Key.

parts.label: b
parts.text: Explain Backtracking. Let set S={1,3,4,5} and X=8, we have to find subset sum problem using backtracking approach.

parts.label: a
parts.text: What is stable sorting algorithm? Which of the sorting algorithms we have seen are stable and which are unstable? Give name with explanation.

parts.label: b
parts.text: Suppose character a, b, c, d, e, f has probabilities 0.07, 0.09, 0.12, 0.22, 0.23, 0.27 respectively. Find an optimal Huffman code and draw the Huffman tree. What is the average code length?

parts.label: a
parts.text: Write Merge sort algorithm and sort the following sequence {23, 11, 5, 15, 68, 31, 4, 17} using merge sort.

parts.label: b
parts.text: Explain B-Tree and its properties. Also write B-Tree deletion cases with example.

parts.label: a
parts.text:
For a binary tree T, the preorder and in-order traversal sequences are as follows:
In order : BCAEGDHFIJ
Preorder : ABCDEGFHIJ
i) Construct a binary Tree.
ii) What is its post-order traversal sequence?

parts.label: b
parts.text: Prove that if the weights on the edge of the connected undirected graph are distinct then there is a unique Minimum Spanning Tree. Give an example in this regard. Also discuss Kruskal's Minimum Spanning Tree in detail.
